text.title: ES Versioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns when evolving event schemas + handling PII. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Developer renames `customer_id` to `buyer_id` in an event class without bumping version. New deploy hits a stream containing both old + new shapes; deserializer fails on the old events.
antipattern.fix: Revert the rename. Add new version with old field name preserved in payload schema; ship an upcaster that maps old → new. Update catalog.

antipattern.symptom: Event class is bumped to `event_version=2`, but no upcaster is registered. Aggregate load reads v1 event, encounters unknown field, raises.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer bumped version without writing the upcaster.
antipattern.fix: Add `upcaster_v1_to_v2(event_v1) -> event_v2` and register it. Add test that loads a v1 fixture and asserts upcast to v2.

antipattern.symptom: Event payload contains `email`, `phone`, `card_token` directly. GDPR erasure request arrives; team cannot satisfy it without rewriting the immutable event log.
antipattern.root-cause: PII written to event log without crypto-shredding or externalization.
antipattern.fix: Choose policy: (a) encrypt PII per-subject; store key in mutable keystore; drop key on erasure (crypto-shredding); OR (b) replace PII fields with opaque IDs and keep PII in a mutable side table. Migrate existing events via an upcaster that masks PII.

antipattern.symptom: Reviewer misses a silent event change in a 1000-line PR. Production deploys; downstream consumers fail; rollback triggered.
antipattern.root-cause: No CI gate enforcing catalog + code agreement.
antipattern.fix: Add CI step: parse event-catalog.yml + introspect event classes; fail if any field set differs from the catalog without a corresponding version bump + upcaster.
